#  Unbounded   
Quantum Computing
Distributed by Design
## Distributed Quantum Computing   
for Breakthrough Applications at Scale
Photonic is building the world’s first commercial-scale, fault-tolerant quantum computer, powered by a unique qubit technology and designed for unlimited scalability from the start. Scaling quantum computers is the grand challenge—not just achieving quality, but quantity—because only at scale can we unlock exponential speedups for the world’s most critical problems, from drug discovery and climate modelling to secure communications and advanced AI.
At the core is Photonic’s Entanglement First™ architecture, which prioritizes entanglement distribution across nodes. Systems are engineered to scale seamlessly and deliver the performance required for breakthrough quantum applications. This architecture eliminates traditional and quantum-specific bottlenecks that limit scale.
#### Commercially Valuable   
Endless scale
Photonic's Entanglement First Architecture delivers thousands of stable, error-corrected qubits working together.
[Learn more](https://photonic.com/technology/entanglement-first/)
#### High-Performance   
Cost-Effective Qubit Platform
T centres in silicon form the ideal qubit platform, providing native integration of compute, memory, and communications. 
[Learn more](https://photonic.com/technology/qubit-platform/)
#### Any-to-Any Connectivity  
Distributed Quantum Computing
Seamless integration of processors over secure quantum optical networking supports modular systems. 
[Learn more](https://photonic.com/technology/networking/)
#### Fast and Lean Error  
Correction Fault Tolerant Computing
High connectivity enables QLDPC codes—Photonic developed SHYPS, a code family capable of performing efficient quantum logic and error correction. 
[Learn more](https://photonic.com/technology/error-correction/)
Our approach
### Architected to  
Scale up, Scale out, Scale performance
Photonic was founded on a simple conviction: conventional quantum computing architectures cannot scale to commercial utility. Achieving that requires a fundamentally different approach—one designed for scale, from the start, and at every level. Photonic’s Entanglement First Architecture scales up via dense qubit integration in a single integrated-silicon module, scales out through distributed computing across telecom-networked modules, and scales performance with fast quantum operations and efficient error correction across thousands of stable qubits.
[Learn about our technology](https://photonic.com/technology/)
Products & SERVICES
### Quantum Computing   
Services, Systems, and Networks
Enterprises, governments, and academia will be able to access Photonic’s Quantum Computing through global cloud-based services on Microsoft Azure, or with dedicated private systems. Quantum Secure Networks will be enabled by Photonic’s quantum networking technology, which supports distributed, modular quantum computing and provides the foundation for Quantum Key Distribution and Quantum Repeaters. These capabilities will be integrated into communications networks and the quantum internet to deliver secure connectivity for governments, telecom providers, and financial institutions.
T CENTRE QUBIT
### A Unique Qubit Modality
The T centre provides the foundation for commercial-scale quantum computing systems and quantum networking. Photonic’s Entanglement First™ architecture leverages the T centre’s native compute, communication, and memory capability for performant, distributed quantum computing.
[Learn about T centres](https://photonic.com/blog/what-is-a-t-centre/)

## View from Industry Experts
**The takeaway: With Microsoft's partnership, Photonic Inc. isn't just accelerating quantum computing—it's reshaping the future of technology.**  
  

Quantum enthusiasts and investors alike should watch this space closely. The quantum race isn't just heating up; it's being redefined.
  * #### Global Quantum Intelligence

![](https://photonic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/gqi-logo.png)
**Photonic proposes an aggressive yet entirely possible roadmap to deliver FTQC well ahead of current market expectations.  
  
**
Photonic offers a unique quantum modality (spin-photon qubits) as the foundation for scalable, distributed, fault tolerant QC systems. Photonic’s core technology offers a plausible shortcut to large-scale fault-tolerant quantum computing.
  * #### Bob Sorenson
Hyperion

![](https://photonic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Hyperion-Research-Logo.png)
**Photonic has a highly disruptive technology approach. Photonic’s silicon spin qubits with optical photonic interconnects hold the enticing prospect of synergies in quantum communications and networking.**  
  

These recent demonstrations are evidence on the way forward. The future path to 200kHz for distributed entanglement with 99.8% fidelity is very striking. This would enable a wide variety of applications. This sets a new bar for quantum roadmaps that others will be under pressure to follow. This stands to accelerate the industry.
  * #### David Shaw
Global Quantum Intelligence

![](https://photonic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/gqi-logo-300x178.png)
With Photonic's architecture you get a 90 percent-plus efficiency of transferring the quantum state from electron to photon. That’s why I think it will become the dominant quantum computing architecture.
  * #### Dr. Hermann Hauser KBE
Co-founder of Acorn Computers (original developers of ARM CPU), serial entrepreneur, and co-founder of Amadeus Capital Partners

Careers 
### Where Ideas Become Industry   
Join us to Commercialize Quantum
Quantum isn’t built by physicists alone. We’re building a business, turning breakthrough research into real‑world products with a multi‑disciplinary team across software, hardware, photonics, product and project management, operations, marketing, and more. If you’re driven to make a difference, you’ll find full‑career paths, hands‑on problem‑solving, and the chance to ship world‑class technology with a world‑class team.
[Join our team](https://photonic.com/careers/)

<p>Photonic was founded on a simple conviction: conventional quantum computing architectures cannot scale to commercial utility.
